name: Olitiau
description: The Olitiau is a cryptid reported from the rainforests of Cameroon, particularly the Lobéké National Park. It's described as a bat-like creature of immense size, with a wingspan ranging from 10 to 30 feet. Witnesses often report seeing it soaring above the trees, its leathery wings casting a shadow over the jungle floor. Some accounts suggest it has reddish-brown fur or skin, while others depict it as entirely black. The Olitiau is often associated with bad omens and is feared by local communities. While some believe it's a surviving Pterosaur or a gigantic species of bat, scientific evidence remains elusive. Its existence persists primarily in folklore and anecdotal sightings, making it a significant figure in Cameroonian cryptozoology and a symbol of the mysteries hidden within the dense rainforests. The creature is said to emit a high-pitched shriek or a series of clicks and whistles, adding to its terrifying presence. It is believed to be nocturnal, emerging from its daytime roosting place to hunt or patrol its territory. Encounters with the Olitiau are rare but deeply impactful, often leaving witnesses shaken and convinced of the existence of something extraordinary lurking in the unexplored depths of the jungle. Its reputation as a harbinger of misfortune further contributes to the fear and respect it commands within local traditions.
subtitle: Giant Bat-like Terror of Cameroon
appearance: A massive bat-like creature with a wingspan of 10-30 feet. It is described as having leathery wings, reddish-brown or black fur/skin, and a large, possibly reptilian, head. Some accounts mention sharp claws and teeth.
habitat: Dense rainforests of Cameroon, particularly Lobéké National Park.
behavior: Nocturnal and territorial. Reported to shriek or emit clicking/whistling sounds. Feared by locals and associated with bad omens. Thought to hunt or patrol its territory from high above the trees.

keyMyths.title: The Omen of the Olitiau
keyMyths.description: Seeing the Olitiau is believed to be a sign of impending misfortune or death within the community. Villagers often avoid venturing into the forest at night, fearing an encounter with the creature.

sources.author: Bernard Heuvelmans
sources.work: On the Track of Unknown Animals
sources.description: A classic cryptozoology book mentioning reports of the Olitiau.
sources.text: A classic cryptozoology book mentioning reports of the Olitiau.

sources.author: Karl Shuker
sources.work: The Beasts That Hide From Man
sources.description: Discusses the Olitiau and its possible origins as a surviving pterosaur or giant bat.
sources.text: Discusses the Olitiau and its possible origins as a surviving pterosaur or giant bat.
